alumni.title: Alumni | PES College of Engineering Mandya | Top Engineering college in Karnataka

h6: Remain an active part of PESCE life

paragraphs: The Office of Alumni Engagement supports the PESCE Alumni Affairs, working on behalf of its board of directors, an all-volunteer policy-setting body. The Affairs represents more than 50,000 alumni and is a non-due paying organization. All PESCE graduates automatically become members and are entitled to the quality services and activities provided by the alumni Affairs.

Whether your connections to PESCE through, various special interest networks or other groups, the PESCE Alumni Affairs serves as a source of volunteer guidance, resources, camaraderie and support. Join us for our events and don't hesitate to let us know what you want from your Alumni Affairs. Your interests are important. Together, we will help ensure the excellence that is the source of our pride in PESCE.

The offices of development and alumni engagement along with advancement leadership make up the Division of Advancement team of approximately 7 Coordinators whose works supports every aspects of PESCE mission.

Working with the PESCE Foundation, partners on campus, and the boards of the Foundation and of the Alumni Affairs, the division strengthens the college and elevates the PESCE experience for students, faculty, staff, alumni, donors and everyone who shares a passion for the institute and its continued success.

Advancement and PESCE thrive thanks to gifts of time, talent and financial support from alumni, donors and other friends. In addition to volunteering as mentors and participating at alumni events worldwide, philanthropy is another important and meaningful way our alumni deepen their engagement and stay connected to PESCE.
Thank you for your generosity!

The PESCE Alumni is a dynamic and inclusive body that is recognized as a valuable resource by the PESCE community. Dedicated coordinators work effectively with campus partners to offer programs that foster campus traditions and perpetuate a passion for a life-long involvement with the PESCE.

The mission of the PESCE Alumni Affairs is to reach, serve and engage all PESCE alumni. The Office of the Alumni Affairs connects PESCE alumni each other and supports the Institutes mission of teaching, research and service. The PESCE Alumni Affairs does this by encouraging, collaboration, inclusiveness and continuing the tradition of excellence and building PESCE pride.
“Students for a short time, alumni for a lifetime”.

Coordinator- Alumni AffairsPh: 9060971110
 E-mail: csunilkumar@pesce.ac.in

The journey of P.E.S. College of Engineering along with you all (Popularly known as PESCE) began in 1962. Completed five decades, initiated Undergraduate and Postgraduate programmes. Today, we all are a strong community of over 50,000 alumni and I would like to take this opportunity to thank every one of you for contributing in making this institute what it is today and at the same time would like to extend an invitation to our strong alumni team to contribute PESCE in academic and research activities for the growth of the institute.

The alumni supported the institute in manifold activities like taking up guest lectures, placements and also as members of Board of Studies to redesign curriculums to match the corporate requirements.

In case you have any suggestions or ideas or would like to share your experiences with us and with our students, you are most welcome. Feel free to contact us anytime or any of the alumni committee members too.
I wish all the best for all your future endeavours!
Graduation doesn’t have to mean the end of your place in the PESCE community.
There are many ways to stay part of PESCE life.
Come back on campus to lend a hand at an open day, mentor or share your expertise through guest workshops.
Combine a challenge or event with raising funds to support the PESCE. More information is available on our fundraising page.

PESCE Alumni Affairs-Overview
The Office of Alumni Engagement supports the PESCE Alumni Affairs, working on behalf of its board of directors, an all-volunteer policy-setting body. The Affairs represents more than 50,000 alumni and is a non-due paying organization. All PESCE graduates automatically become members and are entitled to the quality services and activities provided by the alumni Affairs.
Whether your connections to PESCE through, various special interest networks or other groups, the PESCE Alumni Affairs serves as a source of volunteer guidance, resources, camaraderie and support. Join us for our events and don't hesitate to let us know what you want from your Alumni Affairs. Your interests are important. Together, we will help ensure the excellence that is the source of our pride in PESCE.
The offices of development and alumni engagement along with advancement leadership make up the Division of Advancement team of approximately 7 Coordinators whose works supports every aspects of PESCE mission.
Working with the PESCE Foundation, partners on campus, and the boards of the Foundation and of the Alumni Affairs, the division strengthens the college and elevates the PESCE experience for students, faculty, staff, alumni, donors and everyone who shares a passion for the institute and its continued success.
Advancement and PESCE thrive thanks to gifts of time, talent and financial support from alumni, donors and other friends. In addition to volunteering as mentors and participating at alumni events worldwide, philanthropy is another important and meaningful way our alumni deepen their engagement and stay connected to PESCE.
Thank you for your generosity!
Vision & Mission
Vision
The PESCE Alumni is a dynamic and inclusive body that is recognized as a valuable resource by the PESCE community. Dedicated coordinators work effectively with campus partners to offer programs that foster campus traditions and perpetuate a passion for a life-long involvement with the PESCE.
Mission
The mission of the PESCE Alumni Affairs is to reach, serve and engage all PESCE alumni. The Office of the Alumni Affairs connects PESCE alumni each other and supports the Institutes mission of teaching, research and service. The PESCE Alumni Affairs does this by encouraging, collaboration, inclusiveness and continuing the tradition of excellence and building PESCE pride.
“Students for a short time, alumni for a lifetime”.
Coordinator's Message
Dr. C Sunil Kumar
Coordinator- Alumni Affairs
Ph: 9060971110
E-mail:
csunilkumar@pesce.ac.in
0
Dear Alumni,
The journey of P.E.S. College of Engineering along with you all (Popularly known as PESCE) began in 1962. Completed five decades, initiated Undergraduate and Postgraduate programmes. Today, we all are a strong community of over 50,000 alumni and I would like to take this opportunity to thank every one of you for contributing in making this institute what it is today and at the same time would like to extend an invitation to our strong alumni team to contribute PESCE in academic and research activities for the growth of the institute.
The alumni supported the institute in manifold activities like taking up guest lectures, placements and also as members of Board of Studies to redesign curriculums to match the corporate requirements.
In case you have any suggestions or ideas or would like to share your experiences with us and with our students, you are most welcome. Feel free to contact us anytime or any of the alumni committee members too.
I wish all the best for all your future endeavours!
Thanking You.
Get Involved
Get Involved
Remain an active part of PESCE life
Graduation doesn’t have to mean the end of your place in the PESCE community.
There are many ways to stay part of PESCE life.
Volunteering
Come back on campus to lend a hand at an open day, mentor or share your expertise through guest workshops.
Fundraising
Combine a challenge or event with raising funds to support the PESCE. More information is available on our
fundraising page.

h3: Biography of Dr.B.R. Ravikanthe Gowda IPS

paragraphs: "Sudarshan Mogasale CEO of Dassault Systemes Solutions Lab (formerly 3DPLM). Leader of Dassault Systèmes R&D in India, Dassault Systemes Solutions Lab is part of global R&D centers of Dassault Systèmes contributing to the development of world leading software such as CATIA, ENOVIA, SIMULIA, SolidWorks, DELMIA, GEOVIA, 3DEXCITE etc."
For more Information, Visit: https://in.linkedin.com/in/sudarshan-mogasale-3169614

"Ramachandra .M B.E. in Electrical Power from PES College of Engineering in 1984. Currently Vice President – Operations, Fujitec India Private Limited, Bengaluru.Successfully commenced & established Fujitec Operations for the state of Karnataka in 2008. Established the organisation from scratch and developed strategic partnerships/ alliances with customers. Achieved breakthrough in the market for Hi Rise Projects – Key Accounts – Major Customers of Marke

"Chandan Kumar , mononymously known as 'Chandan', is an actor who works in television and Kannada cinema. He is known for his work in the television soap opera Radha Kalyana and later Lakshmi Baramma, in which he played the role of Chandu, that was aired on Colors Kannada from 2012. B.E. in Mechanical Engineering from PES College of Engineering in 2007."
For more Information, Visit: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chandan_Kumar

"Nagashekhar, is an Indian actor, director and producer who works in the Kannada film industry. He is most predominantly known for his directional movies like Aramane, Sanju Weds Geetha, Mynaa and Maasthi Gudi. B.E. in Enivronmental Engineering from PES College of Engineering in 1994."
"Dr.B.R. Ravikanthe Gowda IPS, (@AddlCPTraffic)."

"Basaral C Srikanta Squadron Leader Basaral C Srikanta (Retd), former Project Director (Programme Management) of Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA), Ministry of Defence, Government of India is an Alumnus of PES College of Engineering, Mandya.He acquired Bachelor of Engineering (Electrical) degree from University of Mysore, Mysore in 1971.Specialties: Aircraft Design & Development; Programme/Project Management; Aircraft maintenance & Integrated Logistics Support (ILS); Technical instruction; Authoring & Courseware development; High value complex contract terms evaluation, techno-commercial negotiations & performance monitoring; Executive level communication & Government liaisons."

"Hemanth M P Motto: Nation first then comes the rest. It gives me immense satisfaction facing new challenges everyday and aiding to safeguard the internal sovereignty of our country. The organisation has given me good opportunity to travel all over the country and work in various states namely Delhi, Kolkata, Sikkim and presently Kerala. Having worked there, I’ve gotten acquainted with the native culture, cuisine, people, language etc.B.E in Information Science and Engineering, Batch - 2005-2009"

"Umesh Chandra B.E. (Mech. Engg.) from PES College of Engineering, Mandya, Mysore University, MBA (Marketing) from Central College, Bangalore University and M.Tech (Aeronautical Maintenance & Production) ENSICA, Toulouse, France. I am an Advisory Panel member of NASSCOM and Life Time member of Indo French Technical Association. I am also a FELLOW of the Institution of Engineers, Fellow of Institution of valuers And Member All India Management Association. I have 34 years of work experience in Engineering Management & Execution with Domain knowledge of Aerospace & Automotive + experience of IT & ITES Industry too. Presently working in BEML Limited ( a Defence PSU under Ministry of Defence, Govt. of India) as Executive Director – Special Projects."

"Dr.Girish Bharadhwaj B Challenging construction of suspension bridges, Training & Development in Rural Connectivity. Qualification- B.E. in Mechanical Engineering (I Class) from P.E.S. College, Mandya of Mysore University in 1973. Fire Fighting Training of M.C.E.F. (Mobile Civil Emergency Force) at New Delhi, 1988. “PADMA SHRI” Award by Government of India on 13th April 2017. "

"Nat Pennathur Senior Vice President at Cit Software solutions, expert with more than 15 years of experience in the IT industry as a developer, consultant or architect. Currently involved in the practice of TOGAF (The Open Group Architecture Framework) for various project initiatives. Proficient in the design, development and delivery of robust and scalable software applications. Specialize in Data Modeling, SOA (Service Oriented Architecture), .Net framework, Web, Security, ETL (Extract Transform Load) and BI (Business Intelligence) solutions. Keen team player with a proven ability to take up unique challenges independently. He pursued his Engineering in Computer Science and Engineering batch of 1985-1989, PES College of Engineering

"Suhas Talak Vice President at JPMorgan Chase & Co. Columbus, Ohio, United States. Solutions architect with experience in designing technology solutions to solve complex business problems. He had led several teams in the delivery effort of best engineering solutions across banking, insurance, telecom and customer relationship management domains. He pursued his Engineering in Computer Science and Engineering batch of 1985-1989, PES College of Engineering 1998-2002

"Ravipakash B V Co-Founder & Director at Ascentive Corporate Learning Solutions Pvt Ltd, Thane, Maharashtra, India. He positioned has Vice President of IOT infrastructure and energy Services Ltd for a period of 6years , he also been Executive director and DGM for Bharath Petroleum for a span of 24 years. He obtained his Bachelors degree in Mechanical Engineering batch of 1979, PES College of Engineering, Mandya.

"Shivakumar N Vice President-Engineering / Facilities at Columbia Asia Hospitals Pvt Ltd Bangalore Urban, Karnataka, India, He pursued his Bachelors degree in Mechanical Engineering from the batch 1997, PESCE. He also positioned as Deputy Chief in Taj Hotels Engineering and Operations for 4 years.

"Sheela Prabhakar Vice President Ittiam Systems Pvt Ltd. Her expertise with Product Engineering and Business management been excellent in field of Engineering. She been positioned with role of Business Manager for Industrial and Defence Solutions. Her expertise in Media streaming solutions had contributed best in IT. She Completed her BE for Electronics and Communication in the year 1984 from PESCE and obtained Masters degree from IISc, Bangalore in the year 1996. She also worked as lecturer in Electronics department at PESCE.

"Sudhakar (Mohandas) Kini Country Head - India at Axa Matrix Risk Consultants, Bangalore Urban, Karnataka, India. He pursued his Bachelors degree in Mechanical Engineering from the batch 1992, PESCE. He acquired his MBA Degree from Institute of Charted Financial Analysts of India (ICFAI) – Dehradun Field Of Study Risk and Insurance in the year 2006.

"Gururaj. K. Jagannath Gururaj Vice President - Integrated Data Solutions | Data Migration & Transformations | Data Management Charlotte, North Carolina, United States. He is Disciplined Senior Technical Manager in Enterprise Data Architecture, Data Warehousing, and Big Data analytics within the financial services industry. Demonstrated skills in information management, software development, and virtualization. Designed and Implemented several large data warehouse projects utilizing a variety of traditional and Agile methodologies to provide a full range of enterprise services and solutions. Proven experience in team management, training, coaching, and mentoring high-performance global teams. Exceptional analytical and critical reasoning skills with the ability to provide innovative solutions. Self-motivated and curious professional possessing the ability to learn quickly and a desire to learn emerging technologies and stay abreast of industry trends. He pursued his Engineering in Computer Science and Engineering, PES College of Engineering 2000-2004.

"Rathnaprabha is a Senior Vice President, Head of Information Technology Risk, Referentials and Finance at Société Générale, and Senior Project Manager at Fidelity Investments. Rathnaprabha Manickavachagam joined Societe Generale in 2015. Rathnaprabha served as Senior Vice President, Head of Digital Innovation and Information Technology Transformation and Director, Innovation and Digital Transformation for Societe Generale. Rathnaprabha Manickavachagam has a Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Communication from PESCE in 1996 and a Master of Business Administration in Finance Managementgrade from Indira Gandhi National Open University in 2014.

"Manu Thejaswi Jayaram Assistant Commandant at Central Reserve Police Force Mandya, Karnataka, India. He pursued his Engineering in Computer Science and Engineering, PES College of Engineering 2005-2009. As a KPSC/UPSC aspirant, he always visioned of becoming an administrator for the amelioration of civil services.

"Manjunath Jayaram Director at NanoCell Networks, since 2009 An Engineer & MBA by Training, 19 years into industry exposure his diverse portfolio includes Marketing, Training, Sales, Strategic Planning and Business Development for companies such as touchtel, Tata Tele Services, Airtel, Hutch, Vodafone and RJio. He has a passion for Education Entrepreneurship involving Training Management, Operations, Business Development, End to End HR, P & L of the firm. His deep technical background and management principles has lead organisations for better resource allocation, great delivery methods and to profitable mode. He is also a Motivated team player with excellent public speaking ability; Let it be Oral, Written or Interpersonal Communication, he stands out ! An Attentive listener who is not afraid to incorporate the opinions of valued team members into policy and planning. His problem-solving and crisis management in a business scenario using creativity and resourcefulness has been accolade of his profession to overcome organizational obstacles, especially in an international context. He completed his BE Mechanical Engineering 1995 – 1999 PESCE, Mandya.

"Ranjan Kumar Co-Founder and Entrepreneur , TestUnity, Having 10+ years of experience in the industry across product strategy, team building and development, I am currently handling client relationships & projects, business development, team building, management, product development and product designing. We have delivered 100+ large, mid-sizes and small projects across different domains & geographies, our clients look up to our company to make their products more stable and compelling. He pursued his MCA from PES College of Engineering 2010.

alumni_chapters.title: Alumni Chapters | PES College of Engineering Mandya | Top Engineering college in Karnataka
paragraphs: This guide aims to help you to establish and manage alumni chapters. Please remember, the Alumni Relations Manager is here to help with practical advice, guidance and tips. We look forward to working with you to help grow the PESCE family.
Alumni chapters are social or professional networking groups organised by and for the PESCE alumni. They help to bring graduates together to participate in local activities and events.

Chapters can be meaningful information hubs, making it easier to share ideas and messages between alumni and the PESCE. They also provide a local contact for alumni, parents, and both current and future students.

Chapters provide an inclusive environment to all alumni and can be based anywhere in the world. Many chapters are organised geographically, aimed at alumni living in a particular city, region or country, but they can also be organised around a particular discipline or department, career, sport, interest and placements.

Some chapters could be both geographical and thematical – e.g a chapter in a city may attract mainly alumni with degrees from a particular school or department. In these cases, chapter organisers should endeavour to find ways to be as inclusive as possible of local alumni from other disciplines. This may include hosting some events that are open to all alumni, while other events are specifically for those alumni from a particular discipline or interest.

PESCE Alumni Affairs - > Alumni Chapters
This guide aims to help you to establish and manage alumni chapters. Please remember, the Alumni Relations Manager is here to help with practical advice, guidance and tips. We look forward to working with you to help grow the PESCE family.
What is an alumni chapter?
Alumni chapters are social or professional networking groups organised by and for the PESCE alumni. They help to bring graduates together to participate in local activities and events.
Chapters can be meaningful information hubs, making it easier to share ideas and messages between alumni and the PESCE. They also provide a local contact for alumni, parents, and both current and future students.
How Chapters work?
Chapters provide an inclusive environment to all alumni and can be based anywhere in the world. Many chapters are organised geographically, aimed at alumni living in a particular city, region or country, but they can also be organised around a particular discipline or department, career, sport, interest and placements.
Some chapters could be both geographical and thematical – e.g a chapter in a city may attract mainly alumni with degrees from a particular school or department. In these cases, chapter organisers should endeavour to find ways to be as inclusive as possible of local alumni from other disciplines. This may include hosting some events that are open to all alumni, while other events are specifically for those alumni from a particular discipline or interest.
The most effective and successful chapters have a regular communication channel with alumni and the Development and Alumni Relations Office.
Click here
to find information regarding Chapter expectations.

h4:
Chief Coordinator-NIRF, Associate Professor Dept. of CS&EMob: +91
 99868 86421

paragraphs:
The National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) was approved by the MHRD and launched by
 Honourable Minister of Human Resource Development on 29th September 2015.

This framework outlines a methodology to rank institutions across the country. The
 methodology draws from the overall recommendations broad understanding arrived at by a Core
 Committee set up by MHRD, to identify the broad parameters for ranking various universities
 and institutions. The parameters broadly cover “Teaching, Learning and Resources,” “Research
 and Professional Practices,” “Graduation Outcomes,” “Outreach and Inclusivity,” and
 “Perception”.

P.E.S. College of Engineering (PESCE), Monday Get Placed 161th Rank in the academic year 2019
 and 147th Rank in Academic Year 2020 & 139th Rank in 2021 make a remarkable change with
 cooperation of faculty members & administrative staff of PESCE family.

The role of the coordinator of NIRF is crucial in Data Gathering presenting the things in
 front of MHRD through DCS By ensuring the effective Representation of all Academic
 Financial, Research & student placement details. Where NIRF Team from MHRD ensures
 correctness of the details submitted by the institute under the all five parameters with
 achieving transparency in system and its concern for achieve excellence by imparting quality
 education.

Acknowledgement Letter by the Govt. Of India for setting high academic
 standards by the Institution under the leadership of Dr. H V Ravindra,
 Principal, PESCE, Mandya.

naac.title: PES College of Engineering Mandya | Top Engineering college in Karnataka
paragraphs:
India has one of the largest and diverse education systems in the world. Privatization, widespread
 expansion, increased autonomy and introduction of Programmes in new and emerging areas have improved
 access to higher education. At the same time, it has also led to widespread concern on the quality
 and relevance of the higher education. To address these concerns, the National Policy on Education
 (NPE, 1986) and the Programme of Action (PoA, 1992) spelt out strategic plans for the policies,
 advocated the establishment of an independent National accreditation agency. Consequently, the
 National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) was established in 1994 as an autonomous
 institution of the University Grants Commission (UGC) with its Head Quarter in Bengaluru. The
 mandate of NAAC as reflected in its vision statement is in making quality assurance an integral part
 of the functioning of Higher Education Institutions (HEIs).

The NAAC functions through its General Council (GC) and Executive Committee (EC) comprising
 educational administrators, policy makers and senior academicians from a crosssection of Indian
 higher education system. The Chairperson of the UGC is the President of the GC of the NAAC, the
 Chairperson of the EC is an eminent academician nominated by the President of GC (NAAC). The
 Director is the academic and administrative head of NAAC and is the member-secretary of both the GC
 and the EC. In addition to the statutory bodies that steer its policies and core staff to support
 its activities NAAC is advised by the advisory and consultative committees constituted from time to
 time.

The vision of NAAC is: To make quality the defining element of higher education in India through
 a combination of self and external quality evaluation, promotion and sustenance initiatives. The
 mission statements of the NAAC aim at translating the NAAC‟s vision into action plans and define
 NAAC‟s engagement and endeavor as given below

Throughout the world, Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) function in a dynamic environment. The
 need to expand the system of higher education, the impact of technology on the educational
 delivery, the increasing private participation in higher education and the impact of
 globalization (including liberal cross-border and trans-national educational imperatives), have
 necessitated marked changes in the Indian higher education system. These changes and the
 consequent shift in values have been taken into cognizance by NAAC while formulating the core
 values. Accordingly, in order to ensure external and internal validity and credibility, the QA
 process of NAAC is grounded within a value framework which is suitable and appropriate to the
 National context.
The accreditation framework of NAAC is thus based on five core values detailed below.

Most of the HEIs have a remarkable capacity to adapt to changes and at the same time,
 pursue the goals and objectives that they have set forth for themselves. Contributing to
 national development has always been an implicit goal of Indian HEIs. The role of HEIs
 is significant in human resource development and capacity building of individuals, to
 cater to the needs of the economy, society and the country as a whole, thereby,
 contributing to the development of the Nation. Serving the cause of social justice,
 ensuring equity and increasing access to higher education are a few ways by which HEIs
 can contribute to the national development. It is therefore appropriate that the
 Assessment and Accreditation (A&A) process of NAAC looks into the ways HEIs have been
 responding to and contributing towards national development.

The spiraling developments at the global level also warrant that the NAAC includes in its
 scope of assessment skill development of students, on par with their counterparts
 elsewhere in the world. With liberalization and globalization of economic activities,
 the need to develop skilled human resources of a high caliber is imperative.
 Consequently, the demand for internationally acceptable standards in higher education is
 evident. Therefore, the accreditation process of NAAC needs to examine the role of HEIs
 in preparing the students to achieve core competencies, to face the global challenges
 successfully. This requires that the HEIs be innovative, creative andentrepreneurial in
 their approach. Towards achieving this, HEIs may establish collaborations with
 industries, network with the neighborhood agencies/bodies and foster a closer
 relationship between the “world of competent-learning” and the “world of skilled work”.

Although skill development is crucial to the success of students in the job market,
 skills are of less value in the absence of appropriate value systems. The HEIs have to
 shoulder the responsibility of inculcating desirable value systems among students. In a
 country like India, with cultural pluralities and diversities, it is essential that
 students imbibe the appropriate values commensurate with social, cultural, economic and
 environmental realities, at the local, national and universal levels. Whatever be the
 pluralities and diversities that exist in the country, there is a persisting concern for
 inculcating the core universal values like truth and righteousness apart from other
 values emphasized in the various policy documents of the country. The seeds of values
 such as cooperation and mutual understanding during the early stages of education have
 to be reiterated and re-emphasized at the higher education also through appropriate
 learning experiences and opportunities. The NAAC assessment therefore examines how these
 essential and desirable values are being inculcated in the students, by the HEIs.

Most of the significant developments that one can observe today can be attributed to the
 impact of Science and Technology. While the advantages of using modern tools and
 technological innovations in the day-to-day-life are well recognized, the corresponding
 changes in the use of new technologies, for teaching learning and governance of HEIs,
 leaves much to be desired. Technological advancement and innovations in educational
 transactions have to be undertaken by all HEIs, to make a visible impact on academic
 development as well as administration. At a time when our educational institutions are
 expected to perform as good as their global partners, significant technological
 innovations have to be adopted. Traditional methods of delivering higher education have
 become less motivating to a large number of students. To keep pace with the developments
 in other spheres of human endeavor, HEIs have to enrich the learning experiences of
 their students by providing them with state-of-the-art educational technologies. The
 campus community must be adequately prepared to make use of Information and
 Communication Technology (ICT) optimally. Conscious effort is also needed to invest in
 hardware and to orient the faculty suitably.

In addition to using technology as a learning resource, managing the activities of the
 institution in a technology-enabled way will ensure effective institutional functioning.
 For example, documentation and data management in the HEIs are areas where the process
 of assessment by NAAC has made a significant impact. Moving towards electronic data
 management and having institutional website to provide ready and relevant information to
 stakeholders are desirable steps in this direction. In other words, effective use of ICT
 in HEIs will be able to provide ICT literacy to the campus community, using ICT for
 resource sharing and networking, as well as adoptingICT-enabled administrative
 processes. Therefore, NAAC accreditation would look at how the HEIs have put in place
 their electronic data management systems and electronic resources and their access to
 internal and external stakeholders particularly the student community.

Contributing to nation-building and skills development of students, HEIs should
 demonstrate a drive to develop themselves into centres of excellence. Excellence in all
 that they will contribute to the overall development of the system of higher education
 of the country as a whole. This „Quest for Excellence‟ could start with the assessment
 or even earlier, by the establishment of the Steering Committee for the preparation of
 the Self - Study Report (SSR) of an institution. Another step in this direction could be
 the identification of the strengths and weaknesses in the teaching and learning
 processes as carried out by the institution.

The five core values as outlined above form the foundation for assessment of institutions that
 volunteer for accreditation by NAAC. The HEIs may also add their own core values to these in
 conformity with the goals and mission.
